fields.description:
Block direct commits to `main`/`master` at commit time by enabling the pre-commit
framework's built-in `no-commit-to-branch` hook, so the "never commit to main"
rule this repo already enforces server-side (GitHub ruleset + Gerrit gates) also
fails fast locally â€” at `git commit` instead of only at push/review.

## Why
CLAUDE.md and CLAUDE.local.md mandate that no change land on `main` except through
Gerrit's two gates. Today nothing stops a local `git commit` while `HEAD` is on
`main`; the mistake is only caught later at push. Catching it at commit time is
cheaper and matches the repo's "errors happen early" posture.

## What
Add the packaged `no-commit-to-branch` hook (id from `pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks`,
already pinned at `rev: v6.0.0` in this repo) to the existing hooks list in
`.pre-commit-config.yaml`, with an EXPLICIT `args: [--branch, main, --branch, master]`.

Rationale for explicit args (chosen over relying on the hook default): the tool
default at v6.0.0 is `frozenset(args.branch or ('master', 'main'))` â€” verified by
inspecting the installed hook source `pre_commit_hooks/no_commit_to_branch.py` â€” so a
bare entry already protects `main`. But passing the branches explicitly (a) documents
intent inline for the next reader, and (b) is robust to any future change of the
upstream default. The cost is two lines; the robustness/clarity gain is worth it.
Because the repo already depends on that pinned repo, this adds no new dependency and
requires no `rev` bump.

## Scope
- One file: `.pre-commit-config.yaml`.
- Add a single `- id: no-commit-to-branch` entry (with the explicit `args`) under the
  existing `- repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks` block (the one
  holding `check-yaml`, `check-toml`, etc.).
- No change to the `local` lint/typecheck hooks; no Makefile change (the hook is
  activated by the existing `pre-commit install` in `make hooks`).
- Non-goal: server-side enforcement (already covered by the GitHub ruleset + Gerrit);
  this is a local fail-fast convenience only, and remains bypassable with
  `git commit --no-verify` by design.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.pre-commit-config.yaml` contains a `no-commit-to-branch` hook id with an
      explicit `args` listing both `main` and `master`, under the
      `pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks` repo block.
      Proving command: `grep -q 'no-commit-to-branch' .pre-commit-config.yaml && python -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('.pre-commit-config.yaml')); hs=[h for r in d['repos'] for h in r['hooks'] if h['id']=='no-commit-to-branch']; a=hs[0].get('args',[]); sys.exit(0 if hs and 'main' in a and 'master' in a else 1)" && echo OK`
- [ ] The config still parses and the hook is registered; running it on the current
      (non-protected) working branch passes.
      Proving command: `pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files` prints
      `Passed` and exits 0 while `HEAD` is on the feature branch.
- [ ] A `git commit` attempted while `HEAD` is on a protected branch is rejected.
      Proving command (negative path, exit code asserted):
      `git switch -c __scratch_main main 2>/dev/null; git branch -f main; git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main; pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files; test $? -ne 0 && echo BLOCKED-OK` â€” i.e. the hook exits non-zero (prints `Failed`) when HEAD is `main`. Restore HEAD to the feature branch afterward. (Run manually in a scratch checkout; not a CI step.)
- [ ] Existing gates are unaffected: `make lint && make typecheck` stay green and the
      YAML hygiene hooks still pass.
      Proving command: `make lint && make typecheck && pre-commit run check-yaml --all-files`
      all exit 0.
